conversation_log.prompt: I want to clone this repo and put it under my name so I can publish it to my own git https://github.iu.edu/I400sp25Vibe/full_stack.git. I am going to be using this github account: https://github.iu.edu/nskotadi. This will be going into the i400sp25Vibe community.

conversation_log.summary: Guided user through cloning the repo, creating a new repo named nskotadi_skating in the I400sp25Vibe org, and pushing. Resolved issues with multi-line URL pasting in zsh terminal and --hostname flag not supported in their gh CLI version.

conversation_log.prompt: The Domain: Sports Management & Logistics. The Goal: Help a local skating school move away from paper tracking and streamline their daily sessions. [Full project spec with MVP features: auth for admins/kids/instructors, class management, attendance, skill levels. Additional features: make-up sessions, parent management, feedback cards, print reports, rink navigation, instructor ratings, ice show with calendar invites for parents].

conversation_log.summary: Explored the existing codebase (community classes template: React 19 + Vite + Express + Supabase). Read the IceSkating Materials.md file for domain context. Designed a full implementation plan including: 18-table PostgreSQL schema, modular backend router structure, React Router v6 frontend with role-based dashboards, 9 build phases (MVP first), calendar integration approach (iCal, Google, Apple, Outlook), and Phase 1 assignment deliverables with cost analysis.

conversation_log.prompt: Both - kids can log in with their own accounts AND parents have accounts. MVP first.

conversation_log.summary: Implemented Phase 0 + Phase 1: replaced community classes schema with 18-table skating school schema seeded with Basic 1-8 levels and skills. Built modular backend (middleware/auth.ts, routers/auth.ts, admin/users.ts, admin/levels.ts). Rebuilt frontend with React Router, AuthContext (localStorage token), role-based Shell nav, LoginPage with 4 roles, Admin dashboard + Users + Levels CRUD pages, placeholder dashboards for instructor/student/parent. Added 6 unit tests for generateClassDates() using Node built-in test runner. Created BLUEPRINT.md.

conversation_log.prompt: Update the readme with relevant info about the project, also include how my part is add ability for parents to get calendar invites in their selected platform for their kids classes.

conversation_log.summary: Rewrote README.md with: project overview, roles table, Nick's calendar feature section (iCal download, Apple webcal subscription, Google Calendar deep link, Outlook deep link, tokenized persistent feed), full data model table of all 18 tables, API reference, setup instructions, and deployment guide.

Checked that Supabase CLI (v2.75.0) and Vercel CLI (v50.23.2) were installed and authenticated. Added dotenv/config import to middleware/auth.ts to fix ESM module initialization order issue. Verified API health check passed locally. Deployed frontend to Vercel (https://web-three-mocha-90.vercel.app). Guided Render setup via render.yaml Blueprint.

conversation_log.prompt: I can't login to my IU GitHub on Render.

conversation_log.summary: Explained that Render only supports github.com, not GitHub Enterprise. Helped user create a github.com repo (nickskotadis/nskotadi_skating) and push to it. User had to run commands from the project directory (was in home directory).

conversation_log.prompt: Deploy failed. [Render logs showing: Cannot find module 'cors', Cannot find module 'express', node:test module errors]

conversation_log.summary: Diagnosed two issues: (1) render.yaml build command missing 'npm install', (2) dates.test.ts using node:test was included in tsc compilation causing type errors. Fixed by updating render.yaml buildCommand to 'npm install && npm --workspace apps/api run build' and adding exclude: ['src/**/*.test.ts'] to tsconfig.json. Verified local build passed, pushed fix to both remotes.

Verified Render health check returned {status: ok}. Set VITE_API_BASE_URL env var on Vercel pointing to Render URL. Redeployed Vercel. Updated local .env CORS_ORIGINS to include Vercel domain. Configured git origin remote to push to both IU GitHub and github.com simultaneously.

Logged into correct Supabase account via CLI. Attempted 'supabase db execute --file' but flag not available in v2.75.0. Used migration approach: created supabase/migrations/20260406000000_initial.sql and ran 'supabase db push --linked'. Hit error: 'query returned more than one row' from seed data — INSERT of 8 rows used RETURNING id INTO scalar variable. Fixed by removing the RETURNING clause (the SELECT statements below it already fetched the IDs). Re-pushed migration successfully. All 18 tables created with Basic 1-8 seed data.

security_audit.prompt: What are 3 worst-case scenarios if the app is not secure? What are 3 risks the app is not prone to due to its domain?

worst_case_scenarios.description: The signup endpoint currently accepts a 'role' parameter for demo purposes. In production, a malicious user could sign up as 'admin' and gain full access to all user data, session management, and the ability to delete records.
worst_case_scenarios.fix: Remove 'role' from the public signup payload. Roles should only be assigned by existing admins via PATCH /api/admin/users/:id. The code includes a comment flagging this for production.

worst_case_scenarios.risk: Broken object-level authorization on parent routes
worst_case_scenarios.description: A parent who guesses a valid UUID could call /api/parent/children/:studentId/skills and retrieve skill data for a child they are not linked to. This exposes another family's child's progress and personal information.
worst_case_scenarios.fix: All parent-facing endpoints must JOIN through parent_student_links verifying parent_id = auth.user.id. Supabase RLS policies add a second enforcement layer.

worst_case_scenarios.risk: Instructor marks attendance for sessions they don't teach
worst_case_scenarios.description: Without session ownership checks, any authenticated instructor could mark a student absent in another instructor's class, triggering unwanted makeup requests and disrupting the school's scheduling.
worst_case_scenarios.fix: The attendance endpoint verifies the requesting instructor's user ID exists in session_instructors for that session before accepting any writes.

non_applicable_risks.reason: Not a threat. All database access goes through @supabase/supabase-js (PostgREST), which auto-parameterizes all queries. There is no raw SQL string concatenation anywhere in the codebase.
Not a threat. React renders all dynamic strings as text nodes, not innerHTML. User-supplied content (instructor notes, parent comments, skill names) cannot inject executable scripts.

Not a threat. The API uses stateless JWT Bearer tokens sent in the Authorization header, not cookies. Browsers do not automatically attach Authorization headers on cross-origin requests, making CSRF structurally impossible.

session_2_build.summary: Completed all 6 remaining feature groups — full SkateTrack implementation

pages/parent: ChildSchedulePage, ChildSkillsPage, ChildFeedbackPage, RatePage, CalendarPage

split grid columns 2fr 1fr -> 1fr 1fr for equal first/last name inputs
added page-header-row flex class (title + button overlap fix)
dynamic tab titles: SkateTrack — Page Name via Shell useEffect + useLocation

session_2_build.deployment_note: Vercel auto-deploy from GitHub broken (root dir mismatch). Must run 'vercel --prod' from apps/web manually. Fix: set Root Directory to apps/web in Vercel dashboard Settings > General.
